stem: A ISP analysis study measures the download speed (Mbps) for a sample of 20 subscribers. The sample mean is 139.8 Mbps with a sample standard deviation of 26.2 Mbps. However, 2 of the 20 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 18 observations have a mean of 134.6 Mbps and standard deviation of 20.4 Mbps. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 139.8, 95% CI = [127.5, 152.1]. Without outliers: mean = 134.6, 95% CI = [125.0, 144.2]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [125.0, 152.1]. Point estimate: 137.2 Mbps.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [125.0, 152.1].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
